notes: UT99 has no native rcon, so control is split across two transports the sidecar speaks over loopback. READS use GameSpy v1 on the query port (game port + 1): unauthenticated, read-only, backing the queries below. WRITES use the UWeb admin console on 5580 (HTTP Basic auth) — the same command interpreter an in-game admin reaches after `adminlogin`, verified against roemer/ut99-server. Commands are sent verbatim through that console; the reply is the recent console-log tail, so a command's effect is best confirmed with a follow-up query (e.g. `server_info` after `change_map`). The map-change verb is `servertravel`, NOT `switchlevel` (which is a no-op through the web console). Finer match settings go via servertravel URL params through the `rcon` escape hatch, e.g. `servertravel CTF-Face.unr?game=Botpack.CTFGame?timelimit=20?mutator=Pkg.Mut`. The shipped mutators are mostly passive (FlagAnnouncementsV2, ZeroPingPlus103, NoSelfDamage, KickIdlePlayers2, WhoPushedMe) or player-side (CustomCrossHairScale `mutate ch_scale`, MVE `!v`) and have no admin control surface; the `rules` query surfaces which are active. The query port exposes no IPs. ADMIN SURFACE — what is PROVEN vs what is NOT. The UWeb console runs `Level.ConsoleCommand`. PROVEN WORKING by observed effect: `servertravel` (map changes), `set`/`get` (value reads back), `say` (appears as 'Admin:'). UNVERIFIED: `Kick`, `AddBots`, `KillAll`, `Pause`, `Mutate` — the UT99 source says these are `exec function`s on PlayerPawn and the web console has no PlayerPawn, so they may silently do nothing; but this could NOT be confirmed, because bots do not spawn on an empty server (UT99 waits for a human before starting the match) and GameSpy `numplayers`/`\players\` do not count bots anyway. They are kept, flagged `unverified`. ⚠️ CRITICAL: the console gives NO feedback. A pure garbage command returns the SAME '(command sent; web admin returned no console output)' as `servertravel`. So that reply is NEVER evidence a command worked — ALWAYS confirm by effect (a follow-up query). NOTE: UE1 `set` writes the CLASS DEFAULT, so a cvar takes effect on the NEXT map — pair it with `change_map` to apply it now.

modData.name: CustomCrossHairScale
modData.notes: Players adjust crosshair size with `mutate ch_scale N`.

modData.name: MapVoteExtended
modData.notes: Post-map vote; players open it with the `!v` chat command.

modData.name: VoteSys
modData.notes: Alternative voting system (image marks it as testing).

modData.name: MonsterHunt
modData.notes: Co-op. Select with change_gametype gametype=MonsterHunt.MonsterHunt on an MH-* map.

modData.name: BunnyTrack
modData.notes: Bunny-hop mode; use its CTF-BT-* maps.

commands.name: change_map
commands.description: Load a map, keeping the current game type. Map prefix must match the running type (a CTF-* map in CTF, DM-* in DeathMatch). Use change_gametype to also switch type.

map.description: Map name without extension, e.g. CTF-Face or DM-Deck16][

commands.name: change_gametype
commands.description: Load a map AND switch the game type. This is the runtime path into the mod game types (MonsterHunt). Map prefix should match the type (CTF-* for CTFGame, MH-* for MonsterHunt, DM-* for DeathMatch/TeamGame/Domination).

map.description: Map name without extension, e.g. MH-Canyon

gametype.description: Game type class: Botpack.CTFGame (CTF), Botpack.DeathMatchPlus (DM), Botpack.TeamGamePlus (Team DM), Botpack.Domination, Botpack.Assault, MonsterHunt.MonsterHunt (co-op mod).

player.description: Exact player name as shown by the players query.

commands.name: add_bots
commands.description: Add bots to the current match.

commands.name: remove_bots
commands.description: Remove all bots from the current match.

commands.name: say
commands.description: Broadcast an admin message to everyone on the server (shown as 'Admin: <message>').

commands.name: pause
commands.description: Toggle pause on the current match.

commands.name: mutate
commands.description: Send a raw server-side mutator command (mutator-specific; escape hatch for mods that expose a mutate verb).

queries.name: server_info
queries.description: Server name, current map, game type and player count, from the GameSpy \info\ query.

queries.name: rules
queries.description: Match settings and active gameplay mutators, from the GameSpy \rules\ query. `mutators` is a comma-separated list of the mutators the running game type reports (e.g. InstaGib) — use it to see which shipped mods are live. The rule set varies by game type.

queries.name: players
queries.description: Human players (name, score, ping, team) from the GameSpy \players\ query. Verified live: bots do NOT appear here however many are in the match, and a player drops from the list while dead/respawning, so treat this as a live snapshot of connected humans, not a roster. The query port exposes no IPs.

cvars.name: time_limit
cvars.description: Match time limit in minutes. Takes effect on the next map (UE1 `set` writes the class default).

cvars.name: goal_team_score
cvars.description: Captures needed to win (CTF). Takes effect on the next map (UE1 `set` writes the class default).

cvars.name: frag_limit
cvars.description: Frags needed to win (DM). Takes effect on the next map (UE1 `set` writes the class default).

cvars.name: friendly_fire
cvars.description: Team-damage scale, 0.0 = off, 1.0 = full. Takes effect on the next map (UE1 `set` writes the class default).

cvars.name: min_players
cvars.description: Bots are spawned to fill the server up to this count. Takes effect on the next map (UE1 `set` writes the class default).

cvars.name: max_players
cvars.description: Player slots. Takes effect on the next map (UE1 `set` writes the class default).

cvars.name: use_translocator
cvars.description: Translocator on/off (True/False). Takes effect on the next map (UE1 `set` writes the class default).

cvars.name: novice_mode
cvars.description: Easier bots (True/False). Takes effect on the next map (UE1 `set` writes the class default).
